title: Alsine subulata
intro: Alsine subulata, also known as dwarf crested-cow-wheat or Alsine, is a low-growing, mat-forming plant species belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to promote wound healing and soothe digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Alsine subulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Alsine subulata is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although it is less commonly used in modern herbal practices due to its limited availability and potential toxicity when consumed in large quantities.
taxonomy: Alsine subulata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This family is commonly known as the carnation or pink family. Alsine subulata is further classified as a member of the genus Alsine, which includes several species of low-growing, creeping plants. The genus Alsine is often associated with acidic soils and temperate regions. Alsine subulata is native to Europe and Asia.
uses: Alsine subulata, also known as dwarf crested-cudweed, is a species of flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Alsine subulata.
